confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_e06bcad8-9dfa-4c87-8057-df22c628256c read back as received

payload.authority_note: Operator explicitly identified the workboard transcript as possible token waste and asked to submit if so.

observations.detail: A normal workboard call produced roughly 428 transcript lines, including repeated source envelopes, closure semantics, review requirements, and concurrency boundaries. This burns context when the architect only needs pending reviews, in-progress tasks, local followups, and blockers.
observations.finding: Workboard JSON is too verbose for routine architect-loop checks
observations.impact: Operator and agent context is spent on stable boilerplate instead of decisions, task review, and actual work.

observations.detail: The full JSON is appropriate for tooling, but the architect duties loop needs a compact, stable summary shape with optional expansion flags.
observations.finding: Machine-readable and operator-loop-readable surfaces are being conflated
observations.impact: Agents either waste tokens by dumping full JSON or risk missing key state by ad hoc filtering.

requests.text: Add a compact workboard mode, e.g. narada task workboard --format compact-json or --view architect-loop, containing only counts, pending reviews, in-progress tasks, local followups, deferred blockers, and high-priority diagnostics.
Move stable guidance such as closure semantics, review handoff requirements, and concurrency boundaries behind explicit --include-guidance or --verbose flags for routine loop use.
Provide a single recommended command for architect next-loop checks that is compact by construction and safe for chat transcript use.

payload.summary: During operator/architect work, narada task workboard --format json emitted hundreds of lines for a routine status check. The information is valuable, but the default loop needs a compact task-oriented surface that avoids dumping repeated guidance into chat context.
payload.title: Add compact architect-loop workboard output to avoid context burn
